_about: Vulture AI bootstrap manifest. setup/install.py reads this to rebuild the whole environment from a fresh clone: ComfyUI + venv, custom nodes, model files and Ollama models. Everything is idempotent -- anything already present is skipped.

_schema.models[]: { name, source, target_base, target_relative_path, approx_size_mb, license_note, required }
_schema.source.type: one of: hf_file | hf_snapshot | url | ollama
_schema.target_base: one of: comfy_models | comfy_custom_nodes | comfy_root | output | ollama  (resolved to a real folder from config.json)
_schema.required: true = core feature depends on it; false = optional / heavy / feature-specific

_paths_note: target_relative_path is relative to the resolved target_base. For ollama entries it is the model tag and target_base is 'ollama'.

comfyui._comment: How to create the ComfyUI install + its venv when it is missing. Only used by the 'comfyui' bootstrap step. torch is pinned to the cu121 build that the RTX playbook validated (works on Pascal GTX 10xx through Ada RTX 40xx). Blackwell cards (RTX 50xx, compute capability >= 10) need cu128 wheels -- the installer switches to the *_blackwell entries automatically.

custom_nodes.name: ComfyUI-GGUF
custom_nodes.note: FLUX GGUF loaders used by the image generator (wf_flux).

custom_nodes.name: ComfyUI-ReActor
custom_nodes.note: Face-swap. The old Gourieff/comfyui-reactor-node repo was disabled by GitHub (ToS) -> moved to Gourieff/ComfyUI-ReActor. Needs insightface (prebuilt cp311 wheel from Gourieff/Assets) + onnxruntime-gpu, then re-pin numpy==1.26.4. Uses inswapper_128 + buffalo_l (manual, non-commercial).

custom_nodes.name: ComfyUI-LivePortraitKJ
custom_nodes.note: Lip-sync / talking photos. Uses mediapipe -> re-pin numpy 1.26.4 afterwards.

custom_nodes.name: ComfyUI-VideoHelperSuite
custom_nodes.note: Load driver video + combine frames to mp4 (lip-sync). Pulls imageio-ffmpeg (bundled ffmpeg).

custom_nodes.name: ComfyUI-KJNodes
custom_nodes.note: RTX video wave: helpers + Wan black-frame patch. Not used by the current core workflows.

custom_nodes.name: ComfyUI-Impact-Pack
custom_nodes.note: Quality-of-life node pack. Not referenced by current studio.py workflows; install only if you extend them.

custom_nodes.name: comfyui_controlnet_aux
custom_nodes.note: ControlNet preprocessors. Optional (for future poses/depth work).

pip_pins.reason: insightface (ReActor) + mediapipe (LivePortrait) break on numpy>=2. MUST be re-forced after every node install.

models.name: FLUX.1-schnell UNet (Q4_K_S GGUF)
models.license_note: Apache-2.0 (commercial use OK). Base model: black-forest-labs/FLUX.1-schnell.

models.name: FLUX autoencoder (VAE)
models.license_note: Apache-2.0 (freely redistributable). Uses the ungated ffxvs/vae-flux mirror; the official black-forest-labs/FLUX.1-schnell repo is gated (401 without HF login).

models.name: FLUX T5-XXL encoder (Q8_0 GGUF, better on Pascal)
models.license_note: Apache-2.0. Optional: studio.py prefers this over fp8 when present (no fp8 speedup on GTX 10xx).

models.name: DreamShaper v8 (SD1.5)
models.license_note: CreativeML OpenRAIL-M (see model card for restrictions).

models.name: Realistic Vision v6 (SD1.5)
models.license_note: CreativeML OpenRAIL-M. Used as the default checkpoint for img2img / upscale / face-swap.

models.name: ToonYou v6 (SD1.5)
models.license_note: CreativeML OpenRAIL-M. If the HF file name differs, download from Civitai (ToonYou) and drop it in checkpoints/.

models.name: 4x-UltraSharp upscaler
models.manual_note: Save it in this folder named EXACTLY 4x-UltraSharp.pth (rename the download if it differs - Vulture loads it by that name).

models.name: ReActor inswapper_128
models.manual_note: Save it in this folder named EXACTLY inswapper_128.onnx (rename the download if it differs).

models.name: InsightFace buffalo_l face-analysis pack
models.license_note: InsightFace -- NON-COMMERCIAL / research use only. Zip is extracted into models/buffalo_l/.
models.manual_note: Download buffalo_l.zip (v0.7 assets) and UNZIP it - the extracted .onnx files go into this buffalo_l folder (not the .zip itself).

models.name: CodeFormer face-restore
models.license_note: S-Lab License 1.0 -- NON-COMMERCIAL. Used for edge smoothing after face-swap.
models.manual_note: Save it in this folder named EXACTLY codeformer.pth (rename the download if it differs).

models.name: LivePortrait weights (Kijai safetensors)
models.license_note: LivePortrait (KwaiVGI) -- research use; MIT wrapper. The KJ node can also auto-download these on first run.
models.manual_note: Put the .safetensors files into this folder. Skip the .pth the page flags as 'unsafe' - it's an old optional landmark model, not needed.

models.name: Ollama: qwen2.5:3b (Super Fast)
models.license_note: Apache-2.0. Tiny + quick -- runs on a 4GB GPU or plain CPU. The safe default for unknown/weak hardware.

models.name: Ollama: qwen3:14b (Best)
models.license_note: Top general quality. Needs 12GB+ VRAM or a big-RAM CPU rig.

models.name: Ollama: qwen2.5-coder:7b (Coding)
models.license_note: Apache-2.0. Code specialist; also powers the image prompt-optimiser. Runs on ~6GB.

models.name: Ollama: deepseek-r1:7b (Reasoning)
models.license_note: MIT. Step-by-step reasoning / debugging. Runs on ~6GB.
